2603.18385.title: Evolutionarily Stable Stackelberg Equilibrium
2603.18385.paper_summary: We present a new solution concept called evolutionarily stable Stackelberg equilibrium (SESS). We study the Stackelberg evolutionary game setting in which there is a single leading player and a symmetric population of followers. The leader selects an optimal mixed strategy, anticipating that the follower population plays an evolutionarily stable strategy (ESS) in the induced subgame and may satisfy additional ecological conditions. We consider both leader-optimal and follower-optimal selection among ESSs, which arise as special cases of our framework. Prior approaches to Stackelberg evolutionary games either define the follower response via evolutionary dynamics or assume rational best-response behavior, without explicitly enforcing stability against invasion by mutations. We present algorithms for computing SESS in discrete and continuous games, and validate the latter empirically. Our model applies naturally to biological settings; for example, in cancer treatment the leader represents the physician and the followers correspond to competing cancer cell phenotypes.
2603.18385.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個新的解決方案概念，稱為進化穩定的史達克伯格均衡（SESS）。<lb/>我們研究了史達克伯格進化遊戲的設定，其中有一個領導者和一個對稱的追隨者群體。<lb/>領導者選擇一個最佳的混合策略，預期追隨者群體在誘導的子遊戲中採用進化穩定策略（ESS），並可能滿足額外的生態條件。<lb/>我們考慮在ESS之間的領導者最佳選擇和追隨者最佳選擇，這些都是我們框架的特例。<lb/>以往對史達克伯格進化遊戲的研究要麼通過進化動態定義追隨者的反應，要麼假設理性的最佳反應行為，而沒有明確地強制對突變的入侵保持穩定性。<lb/>我們提出了計算SESS的算法，適用於離散和連續遊戲，並對後者進行了實證驗證。<lb/>我們的模型自然適用於生物學環境；例如，在癌症治療中，領導者代表醫生，而追隨者則對應於競爭的癌細胞表型。
2603.18385.author: Sam Ganzfried et.al.
